title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Section Test

questions.question: Which of the following will yield maximum discount on ‚Çπ7,500 ? 1. Two successive discounts of 5% and 5% 2. Single discount of 10% 3. Two successive discounts of 8% and 2%
options: All will yield the same discount
questions.explanation: ![](https://ts-cdn.adda247.com/1721925987449image65.png)

questions.question: ‚ÄãThe bar graph given below represents the production (in tonnes) and sales (in tones) of steel in India from 2009-2014.‚Äã ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/84aacfad-0281-4800-8d47-ac1e767df136.png) ‚ÄãWhat is the difference (in tonnes) in the average sale from 2012 to 2014 and the average production from 2009 to 2014?‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given: Sales (tonnes): 2012 = 500, 2013 = 600, 2014 = 640 Production (tonnes): 2009 = 520, 2010 = 740, 2011 = 700, 2012 = 580, 2013 = 810, 2014 = 760 Find: Difference between average sales (2012‚Äì2014) and average production (2009‚Äì2014). Formula Used: Average =Sum of valuesNumber of years\dfrac{\text{Sum of values}}{\text{Number of years}}Number of yearsSum of values‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution: ‚ÄãAvg Sales2012‚àí14=500+600+6403=17403=\text{Avg Sales}_{2012-14}=\dfrac{500+600+640}{3}=\dfrac{1740}{3}=Avg Sales2012‚àí14‚Äã=3500+600+640‚Äã=31740‚Äã=‚Äã580 tonnes ‚ÄãAvg Production2009‚àí14=520+740+700+580+810+7606=41106=685\text{Avg Production}_{2009-14}=\dfrac{520+740+700+580+810+760}{6}=\dfrac{4110}{6}=685Avg Production2009‚àí14‚Äã=6520+740+700+580+810+760‚Äã=64110‚Äã=685‚Äã tonnes Difference = |580 - 685|= 105 tonnes

questions.question: In a right triangle ABC, right-angled at B, if tan A = ‚àö3, what is the value of 2 sin A cos A?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven:Right triangle ABC with right angle at Btan‚Å°A=3Formula Used:2sin‚Å°Acos‚Å°A=sin‚Å°2ASolution:If tan‚Å°A=3, then A=60‚àòsin‚Å°2A=sin‚Å°(2√ó60‚àò)sin‚Å°2A=sin‚Å°120‚àòsin‚Å°120‚àò=32‚à¥ 2sin‚Å°Acos‚Å°A=32So, the correct answer is (b).\textbf{Given:} \\\text{Right triangle } ABC \text{ with right angle at } B \\\tan A = \sqrt{3}\\\textbf{Formula Used:} \\2 \sin A \cos A = \sin 2A\\\textbf{Solution:} \\\text{If } \tan A = \sqrt{3}, \text{ then } A = 60^\circ\\\sin 2A = \sin(2 \times 60^\circ) \\\sin 2A = \sin 120^\circ\\\sin 120^\circ = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\\\therefore\ 2 \sin A \cos A = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\\\text{So, the correct answer is (b).}Given:Right triangle ABC with right angle at BtanA=3‚ÄãFormula Used:2sinAcosA=sin2ASolution:If tanA=3‚Äã, then A=60‚àòsin2A=sin(2√ó60‚àò)sin2A=sin120‚àòsin120‚àò=23‚Äã‚Äã‚à¥ 2sinAcosA=23‚Äã‚ÄãSo, the correct answer is (b).‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: If x + 1x\frac 1xx1‚Äã‚Äã= ‚Äì1, then compute: x4+1x4+2x2+2x2x^4 + \frac{1}{x^4} + 2x^2 + \frac{2}{x^2}x4+x41‚Äã+2x2+x22‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äãx+1x=‚àí1x + \frac{1}{x} = -1x+x1‚Äã=‚àí1‚Äã‚Äã Formula Used: ‚Äã(x+1x)2=x2+1x2+2(x2+1x2)2=x4+1x4+2\left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^2 = x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} + 2 \\\left(x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2}\right)^2 = x^4 + \frac{1}{x^4} + 2(x+x1‚Äã)2=x2+x21‚Äã+2(x2+x21‚Äã)2=x4+x41‚Äã+2‚Äã‚Äã Solution: x+1x=‚àí1Squaring both sides:(x+1x)2=(‚àí1)2x2+1x2+2=1x2+1x2=‚àí1Squaring again:(x2+1x2)2=(‚àí1)2x4+1x4+2=1x4+1x4=‚àí1Now evaluate:x4+1x4+2x2+2x2=(‚àí1)+2(‚àí1)=‚àí3x + \frac{1}{x} = -1\\[4pt]\text{Squaring both sides:} \\\left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^2 = (-1)^2 \\x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} + 2 = 1 \\x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} = -1\\[6pt]\text{Squaring again:} \\\left(x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2}\right)^2 = (-1)^2 \\x^4 + \frac{1}{x^4} + 2 = 1 \\x^4 + \frac{1}{x^4} = -1\\[6pt]\text{Now evaluate:} \\x^4 + \frac{1}{x^4} + 2x^2 + \frac{2}{x^2} \\= (-1) + 2(-1) \\= -3x+x1‚Äã=‚àí1Squaring both sides:(x+x1‚Äã)2=(‚àí1)2x2+x21‚Äã+2=1x2+x21‚Äã=‚àí1Squaring again:(x2+x21‚Äã)2=(‚àí1)2x4+x41‚Äã+2=1x4+x41‚Äã=‚àí1Now evaluate:x4+x41‚Äã+2x2+x22‚Äã=(‚àí1)+2(‚àí1)=‚àí3‚Äã

questions.question: Ratio of the incomes of 'A' , 'B' and 'C' last year was 3:4:5. The ratio of their individual income of the last year and this year are 4:5, 2:3 and 3:4 respectively. If the sum of their present incomes is Rs.78800/- then find the present individual incomes of 'A', 'B', & 'C' respectively?
questions.explanation: Given: Last-year income ratio A : B : C = 3 : 4 : 5 Year-to-year growth ratios: A = 4 : 5, B = 2 : 3, C = 3 : 4 Present total income = Rs 78,800 Solution: Let the last-year incomes A's last-year income = 3x B's last-year income = 4x C's last-year income = 5x Now, A's present income = 3x√ó54=15x43x \times \frac{5}{4} = \frac{15x}{4}3x√ó45‚Äã=415x‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã B's present income = 4x√ó32=6x4x \times \frac{3}{2} = 6x4x√ó23‚Äã=6x‚Äã‚Äã C's present income = 5x√ó43=20x35x \times \frac{4}{3} = \frac{20x}{3}5x√ó34‚Äã=320x‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã The sum of present incomes is: ‚Äã15x4+6x+20x3=78,800 45x12+72x12+80x12=78,800 197x12=78,800 197x=78,800√ó12 x=945,600197=4800\frac{15x}{4} + 6x + \frac{20x}{3} = 78,800 \\ \ \\\frac{45x}{12} + \frac{72x}{12} + \frac{80x}{12} = 78,800\\ \ \\\frac{197x}{12} = 78,800\\ \ \\197x = 78,800 \times 12 \\ \ \\x = \frac{945,600}{197} = 4800415x‚Äã+6x+320x‚Äã=78,800 1245x‚Äã+1272x‚Äã+1280x‚Äã=78,800 12197x‚Äã=78,800 197x=78,800√ó12 x=197945,600‚Äã=4800‚Äã‚Äã So, A's present income = 15√ó48004=Rs. 18,000 \frac{15 \times 4800}{4} = Rs. \, 18,000415√ó4800‚Äã=Rs.18,000‚Äã‚Äã B's present income = 6√ó4800=Rs. 28,8006 \times 4800 = Rs. \, 28,8006√ó4800=Rs.28,800‚Äã‚Äã C's present income = 20√ó48003=Rs. 32,000\frac{20 \times 4800}{3} = Rs. \, 32,000320√ó4800‚Äã=Rs.32,000 Exam Hall Method: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/0c297346-e2c0-4a6f-b87f-3160220928c2.png)

questions.question: Pipe A can fill a tank in 13 hours, pipe B can fill the same tank in 26 hours and pipe C can fill the same tank in 17 hours. The time taken by them to fill the same tank if they operate together is:
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven:Time taken by Pipe A=13 hoursTime taken by Pipe B=26 hoursTime taken by Pipe C=17 hoursFormula Used:Total Work=LCM of given times1-hour work=Total WorkTimeTime=Total WorkTotal 1-hour workSolution:LCM of 13,26,17=442Total work=442 units1-hour work of Pipe A=44213=34 units 1-hour work of Pipe B=44226=17 units1-hour work of Pipe C=44217=26 unitsTotal 1-hour work together=34+17+26=77 unitsRequired time=44277=44277=55777 hours\textbf{Given:} \\\text{Time taken by Pipe A} = 13 \text{ hours} \\\text{Time taken by Pipe B} = 26 \text{ hours} \\\text{Time taken by Pipe C} = 17 \text{ hours} \\\\\textbf{Formula Used:} \\\text{Total Work} = \text{LCM of given times} \\\text{1-hour work} = \dfrac{\text{Total Work}}{\text{Time}} \\\text{Time} = \dfrac{\text{Total Work}}{\text{Total 1-hour work}} \\\\\textbf{Solution:} \\\text{LCM of } 13, 26, 17 = 442 \\\\\text{Total work} = 442 \text{ units} \\\\\text{1-hour work of Pipe A} = \dfrac{442}{13} = 34 \text{ units} \\\\\ \\\text{1-hour work of Pipe B} = \dfrac{442}{26} = 17 \text{ units} \\\\\text{1-hour work of Pipe C} = \dfrac{442}{17} = 26 \text{ units} \\\\\text{Total 1-hour work together} = 34 + 17 + 26 = 77 \text{ units} \\\\\text{Required time} = \dfrac{442}{77} \\= \dfrac{442}{77} \\= 5 \frac{57}{77} \text{ hours}Given:Time taken by Pipe A=13 hoursTime taken by Pipe B=26 hoursTime taken by Pipe C=17 hoursFormula Used:Total Work=LCM of given times1-hour work=TimeTotal Work‚ÄãTime=Total 1-hour workTotal Work‚ÄãSolution:LCM of 13,26,17=442Total work=442 units1-hour work of Pipe A=13442‚Äã=34 units 1-hour work of Pipe B=26442‚Äã=17 units1-hour work of Pipe C=17442‚Äã=26 unitsTotal 1-hour work together=34+17+26=77 unitsRequired time=77442‚Äã=77442‚Äã=57757‚Äã hours‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: If the difference between the interior and exterior angles of a polygon is 36¬∞, then find the number of sides in the polygon.
questions.explanation: Given: The difference between the interior and exterior angles of a regular polygon is 36¬∞ Formula Used: the Sum of interior and exterior angle = 180¬∞ Solution: Let the interior angle be x¬∞ then, exterior angle will be (x - 36)¬∞ We know that the sum of an interior angle and its corresponding exterior angle is 180 degrees. So, x + (x - 36) = 180¬∞ 2x - 36 = 180¬∞ 2x = 216¬∞ x = 108¬∞ Exterior Angle = (108 - 36)¬∞ = 72¬∞ Each Exterior Angle = 360¬∞Number of sides(n)\frac {360\degree}{\text{Number of sides(n)}}Number of sides(n)360¬∞‚Äã 72¬∞ = 360¬∞Number of sides(n)\frac {360\degree}{\text{Number of sides(n)}}Number of sides(n)360¬∞‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Number of sides(n)=5\text{Number of sides(n)} = 5Number of sides(n)=5‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: A fraction35 \frac3553‚Äã‚Äã is decreased by 25 \frac2552‚Äã‚Äã . The resulting fraction is then increased by 20%. What is the percentage decrease/increase in the value of the original fraction?
questions.explanation: Solution: New fraction when it is decreased by 25\frac{2}{5}52‚Äã‚Äã and then increased by 20% ‚Äã=(35‚àí25)√ó(120100)=15√ó65=625= \left( \frac{3}{5} - \frac{2}{5} \right) \times \left( \frac{120}{100} \right) \\= \frac{1}{5} \times \frac{6}{5} \\= \frac{6}{25}\\=(53‚Äã‚àí52‚Äã)√ó(100120‚Äã)=51‚Äã√ó56‚Äã=256‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Percentage increase and decrease in the value of the original fraction: ‚ÄãPercentage Change=(625‚àí3535)√ó100%=&gt(625‚àí152535)√ó100%=&gt(‚àí925√ó53)√ó100%=&gtDecrease =‚àí60%\text{Percentage Change} = \left( \frac{\frac{6}{25} - \frac{3}{5}}{\frac{3}{5}} \right) \times 100\% \\\Rightarrow \left( \frac{\frac{6}{25} - \frac{15}{25}}{\frac{3}{5}} \right) \times 100\% \\\Rightarrow \left( \frac{-9}{25} \times \frac{5}{3} \right) \times 100\% \\\Rightarrow\text{Decrease =} -60\%\\Percentage Change=(53‚Äã256‚Äã‚àí53‚Äã‚Äã)√ó100%=&gt(53‚Äã256‚Äã‚àí2515‚Äã‚Äã)√ó100%=&gt(25‚àí9‚Äã√ó35‚Äã)√ó100%=&gtDecrease =‚àí60%‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: The monthly income of A, B and C taken together is ‚Çπ69,000. A spends 70% of income, B spends 80% of income and C spends 92% of income. If their monthly savings are in the ratio of 15 : 11 : 10, respectively, then the monthly savings (in ‚Çπ) of B is:
questions.explanation: Given: Total income of A, B, C = ‚Çπ69,000 A saves 30% of income, B saves 20%, C saves 8% Savings ratio (A : B : C) = 15 : 11 : 10 Formula Used: ‚ÄãSaving=Income√óSaving%\text{Saving} = \text{Income} \times \text{Saving\%}Saving=Income√óSaving%‚Äã‚Äã Solution: ‚ÄãLet common factor of savings = k So: A's saving = (15k) = 30% of A‚Äôs income B's saving = (11k) = 20% of B‚Äôs income C's saving = (10k) = 8% of C‚Äôs income a + b + c = 69000 From savings: For A: 0.30a = 15k ‚üπ a=15k0.30=50k \implies a = \frac{15k}{0.30} = 50k‚üπa=0.3015k‚Äã=50k‚Äã‚Äã For B: 0.20b = 11k ‚üπ b=11k0.20=55k11k \implies b = \frac{11k}{0.20} = 55k11k‚üπb=0.2011k‚Äã=55k‚Äã‚Äã For C: 0.08c = 10k ‚üπ c=10k0.08=125kk \implies c = \frac{10k}{0.08} = 125kk‚üπc=0.0810k‚Äã=125k‚Äã‚Äã Now sum incomes a + b + c = 50k + 55k + 125k = 230k Given: 230k = 69000 k =69000230=30= \frac{69000}{230} = 30=23069000‚Äã=30‚Äã0 So B's saving: 11k = 11√ó3001 \times 300 1√ó300‚Äã= 3300 Exam Hall Method: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/db64676b-7273-44a1-8f81-e7912d8f9730.png) ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/a6ee3bd4-bddb-4cba-acb2-507bf1eafdc2.png)

questions.question: A shopkeeper has a sale of Rs. 10220, Rs. 11542, Rs. 8458 and Rs. 9845 for four consecutive weeks. How much sale he must have in the fifth week, so that he gets an average sale of Rs. 10362 per week?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Sales for 4 weeks: W1=10220,W2=11542,W3=8458,W4=9845 Required Average for 5 weeks=10362 Formula Used: Total Sum=Average√óNumber of Weeks Solution: Calculate the total required sales for 5 weeks: Total Sales=10362√ó5=51810 Calculate the sum of sales for the first 4 weeks: Sum=10220+11542+8458+9845=40065 Sales required in the 5th week: W5=Total Sales‚àíSum of 4 weeks W5=51810‚àí40065=11745 Final Answer Rs. 11745\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Sales for 4 weeks:} \\ \space \, \\ W_1 = 10220, W_2 = 11542, W_3 = 8458, W_4 = 9845 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Required Average for 5 weeks} = 10362 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Total Sum} = \text{Average} \times \text{Number of Weeks} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Calculate the total required sales for 5 weeks:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Total Sales} = 10362 \times 5 = 51810 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Calculate the sum of sales for the first 4 weeks:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Sum} = 10220 + 11542 + 8458 + 9845 = 40065 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Sales required in the 5th week:} \\ \space \, \\ W_5 = \text{Total Sales} - \text{Sum of 4 weeks} \\ \space \, \\ W_5 = 51810 - 40065 = 11745 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Rs. } 11745Given: Sales for 4 weeks: W1‚Äã=10220,W2‚Äã=11542,W3‚Äã=8458,W4‚Äã=9845 Required Average for 5 weeks=10362 Formula Used: Total Sum=Average√óNumber of Weeks Solution: Calculate the total required sales for 5 weeks: Total Sales=10362√ó5=51810 Calculate the sum of sales for the first 4 weeks: Sum=10220+11542+8458+9845=40065 Sales required in the 5th week: W5‚Äã=Total Sales‚àíSum of 4 weeks W5‚Äã=51810‚àí40065=11745 Final Answer Rs. 11745‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Solve the following linear equation. 2x ‚àí y + z = 8, x + y ‚àí z = 10, x + y + 2z = 12
questions.explanation: Given: {2x‚àíy+z=8(1)x+y‚àíz=10(2)x+y+2z=12(3)\begin{cases}2x - y + z = 8 \quad \text{(1)} \\x + y - z = 10 \quad \text{(2)} \\x + y + 2z = 12 \quad \text{(3)}\end{cases}‚é©‚é®‚éß‚Äã2x‚àíy+z=8(1)x+y‚àíz=10(2)x+y+2z=12(3)‚Äã‚Äã Solution: (2x - y + z) + (x + y - z) = 8 + 10 3x = 18 x = 6 6 + y - z = 10 y - z = 4 _____(4) ( x = 6) into equation (3) 6 + y + 2z = 12 y + 2z = 6____ (5) From equation (4) y = z + 4 ( y = z + 4) into equation (5) (z + 4) + 2z = 6 3z + 4 = 6 3z = 2 =&gtz=23\quad \Rightarrow \quad z = \frac{2}{3}=&gtz=32‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã ( z = 23\frac{2}{3}32‚Äã‚Äã) into ( y = z + 4) y = 23+4=143\frac{2}{3} + 4 = \frac{14}{3}32‚Äã+4=314‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã x = 6, y=143,z=23\quad y = \frac{14}{3}, \quad z = \frac{2}{3}y=314‚Äã,z=32‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: ‚Çπ1,380 has to be paid after 4 years. If four equal instalments are required with a simple interest of 10% on each instalment, then what is the value of each instalment?
questions.explanation: Given: Principle amount =Rs1380 Rate of interest = 10% Time = 4years Formula Used: ‚ÄãSimple Interest=P√óR√óT100\text{Simple Interest} = \frac{P \times R \times T}{100}Simple Interest=100P√óR√óT‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Solution: Let the installment be x 1st year = x+x√ó10√ó3100x + \frac{x \times 10 \times 3}{100}x+100x√ó10√ó3‚Äã‚Äã 2nd year = x+x√ó10√ó2100x + \frac{x \times 10 \times 2}{100}x+100x√ó10√ó2‚Äã‚Äã 3rd year = x+x√ó10√ó1100x + \frac{x \times 10 \times 1}{100}x+100x√ó10√ó1‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã 4th year = x Then, ‚Äãx+x√ó10√ó3100+x+x√ó10√ó2100+x+x√ó10√ó1100+x=13804x+6x10=138046x=13800x=Rs 300x + \frac{x \times 10 \times 3}{100} + x + \frac{x \times 10 \times 2}{100} + x + \frac{x \times 10 \times 1}{100} + x = 1380\\4x + \frac{6x}{10} = 1380\\46x = 13800\\x = \text{Rs} \ 300x+100x√ó10√ó3‚Äã+x+100x√ó10√ó2‚Äã+x+100x√ó10√ó1‚Äã+x=13804x+106x‚Äã=138046x=13800x=Rs 300 Alternate Solution: Installment=100√óAmount{(100t)+[Rt√ót‚àí12]}\text{Installment} = \frac{100\times \text{Amount}}{\{ (100t) + [ Rt \times \frac{t - 1} 2 ] \}}Installment={(100t)+[Rt√ó2t‚àí1‚Äã]}100√óAmount‚Äã ‚ÄãInstallment=(100√ó1380){(100√ó4)+[10√ó4√ó4‚àí12]}\text{Installment} = \frac{(100 \times 1380)}{\{ (100 \times 4) + [10 \times 4 \times \frac{4 - 1} 2] \}}Installment={(100√ó4)+[10√ó4√ó24‚àí1‚Äã]}(100√ó1380)‚Äã = 300‚Äã

questions.question: A regular right pyramid has a square base of side 14 cm and a vertical height of 24 cm. Find the slant height and the total surface area of the pyramid (approximately).
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven:Side of square base=14Vertical height of pyramid=24Concept Used:Right square pyramid geometryFormula Used:l=h2+(a2)2Lateral surface area=2alBase area=a2Total surface area=Lateral area+Base areaSolution:a2=142=7l=242+72=576+49=625=25Lateral surface area=2√ó14√ó25=700Base area=142=196Total surface area=700+196=896Final Answer:Slant height=25 cmTotal surface area‚âà896 cm2\textbf{Given:}\\\text{Side of square base} = 14\\\text{Vertical height of pyramid} = 24\\[6pt]\textbf{Concept Used:}\\\text{Right square pyramid geometry}\\[6pt]\textbf{Formula Used:}\\l = \sqrt{h^2 + \left(\frac{a}{2}\right)^2}\\\text{Lateral surface area} = 2al\\\text{Base area} = a^2\\\text{Total surface area} = \text{Lateral area} + \text{Base area}\\[6pt]\textbf{Solution:}\\\frac{a}{2} = \frac{14}{2} = 7\\[4pt]l= \sqrt{24^2 + 7^2}\\= \sqrt{576 + 49}\\= \sqrt{625}\\= 25\\[6pt]\text{Lateral surface area}= 2 \times 14 \times 25= 700\\[4pt]\text{Base area}= 14^2= 196\\[4pt]\text{Total surface area}= 700 + 196= 896\\[6pt]\textbf{Final Answer:}\\\text{Slant height} = 25 \text{ cm}\\\text{Total surface area} \approx 896 \text{ cm}^2Given:Side of square base=14Vertical height of pyramid=24Concept Used:Right square pyramid geometryFormula Used:l=h2+(2a‚Äã)2‚ÄãLateral surface area=2alBase area=a2Total surface area=Lateral area+Base areaSolution:2a‚Äã=214‚Äã=7l=242+72‚Äã=576+49‚Äã=625‚Äã=25Lateral surface area=2√ó14√ó25=700Base area=142=196Total surface area=700+196=896Final Answer:Slant height=25 cmTotal surface area‚âà896 cm2‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: Find the ratio of the area of an equilateral triangle to the area of a circle, having the same perimeter. (Use œÄ = 22/7)
questions.explanation: ‚Äã‚Äã‚ÄãGiven: œÄ=227 Concept Used: If two figures have the same perimeter, their areas can be compared using their area formulas. Formula Used: 3a=Perimeter of equilateral triangle 2œÄr=Perimeter of circle A‚ñ≥=34a2 Acircle=œÄr2 Solution: 3a=2œÄr a=2œÄr3 A‚ñ≥=34(2œÄr3)2 =34‚ãÖ4œÄ2r29 =3œÄ2r29 Acircle=œÄr2 Required Ratio=3œÄ2r29:œÄr2 =3œÄ9:1 œÄ=227 Ratio=22363:1 Final Answer: 223:63=22:213\textbf{Given:} \\ \, \\\pi = \frac{22}{7} \\ \, \\\textbf{Concept Used:} \\ \, \\\text{If two figures have the same perimeter, their areas can be compared using their area formulas.} \\ \, \\\textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \, \\3a = \text{Perimeter of equilateral triangle} \\ \, \\2\pi r = \text{Perimeter of circle} \\ \, \\A_{\triangle} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}a^2 \\ \, \\A_{\text{circle}} = \pi r^2 \\ \, \\\textbf{Solution:} \\ \, \\3a = 2\pi r \\ \, \\a = \frac{2\pi r}{3} \\ \, \\A_{\triangle}= \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\left(\frac{2\pi r}{3}\right)^2 \\ \, \\= \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\cdot\frac{4\pi^2 r^2}{9} \\ \, \\= \frac{\sqrt{3}\pi^2 r^2}{9} \\ \, \\A_{\text{circle}} = \pi r^2 \\ \, \\\text{Required Ratio}= \frac{\sqrt{3}\pi^2 r^2}{9} : \pi r^2 \\ \, \\= \frac{\sqrt{3}\pi}{9} : 1 \\ \, \\\pi = \frac{22}{7} \\ \, \\\text{Ratio}= \frac{22\sqrt{3}}{63} : 1 \\ \, \\\textbf{Final Answer:} \\ \, \\22\sqrt{3} : 63= 22 : 21\sqrt3Given:œÄ=722‚ÄãConcept Used:If two figures have the same perimeter, their areas can be compared using their area formulas.Formula Used:3a=Perimeter of equilateral triangle2œÄr=Perimeter of circleA‚ñ≥‚Äã=43‚Äã‚Äãa2Acircle‚Äã=œÄr2Solution:3a=2œÄra=32œÄr‚ÄãA‚ñ≥‚Äã=43‚Äã‚Äã(32œÄr‚Äã)2=43‚Äã‚Äã‚ãÖ94œÄ2r2‚Äã=93‚ÄãœÄ2r2‚ÄãAcircle‚Äã=œÄr2Required Ratio=93‚ÄãœÄ2r2‚Äã:œÄr2=93‚ÄãœÄ‚Äã:1œÄ=722‚ÄãRatio=63223‚Äã‚Äã:1Final Answer:223‚Äã:63=22:213‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: If a shopkeeper bears 25% loss, then which of the following statements is/are correct? Statement A: The cost price of 15 articles is equal to the selling price of 20 articles. Statement B: The cost price of 12 articles is equal to the selling price of 17 articles.
options: Both Statements A and B are correct.
Neither Statement A nor B is correct.
questions.explanation: Given: The shopkeeper bears a 25% loss. Formula Used:‚Äã If the loss percentage is x, then the relationship between cost price (CP) and selling price (SP) is: SP =CP√ó(1‚àíx100)= \text{CP} \times \left(1 - \frac{x}{100}\right)=CP√ó(1‚àí100x‚Äã)‚Äã Solution : For a 25% loss, the selling price is 75% of the cost price: SP =CP√ó75100=CP√ó0.75 \text{CP} \times \frac{75}{100} = \text{CP} \times 0.75CP√ó10075‚Äã=CP√ó0.75‚Äã‚Äã Statement A: The cost price of 15 articles is 15 √ó CP. The selling price of 20 articles is 20 √ó SP = 20 √ó 0.75 √ó CP = 15 √ó CP Statement A is correct. Statement B: The cost price of 12 articles is 12 √ó CP. The selling price of 17 articles is 17 √ó SP = 17 √ó 0.75 √ó CP = 12.75 √ó CP Statement B is incorrect because the cost price of 12 articles is not equal to the selling price of 17 articles. Thus, Statement A is correct, and Statement B is incorrect.

questions.question: If cos Œ∏ = 725\frac{7}{25}257‚Äã‚Äã and Œ∏ lies in Quadrant IV, then find the value of sin Œ∏.
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGivencos‚Å°Œ∏=725Œ∏ lies in Quadrant IVFormula Usedsin‚Å°2Œ∏+cos‚Å°2Œ∏=1Solutionsin‚Å°2Œ∏=1‚àí(725)2sin‚Å°2Œ∏=1‚àí49625sin‚Å°2Œ∏=576625sin‚Å°Œ∏=2425Since Œ∏ lies in Quadrant IVsin‚Å°Œ∏=‚àí2425Final Answer‚àí2425\mathbf{Given} \\\cos \theta = \frac{7}{25} \\\theta \text{ lies in Quadrant IV} \\\mathbf{Formula\ Used} \\\sin^2 \theta + \cos^2 \theta = 1 \\\mathbf{Solution} \\\sin^2 \theta = 1 - \left(\frac{7}{25}\right)^2 \\\sin^2 \theta = 1 - \frac{49}{625} \\\sin^2 \theta = \frac{576}{625} \\\sin \theta = \frac{24}{25} \\\text{Since } \theta \text{ lies in Quadrant IV} \\\sin \theta = -\frac{24}{25} \\\mathbf{Final\ Answer} \\-\frac{24}{25}GivencosŒ∏=257‚ÄãŒ∏ lies in Quadrant IVFormula Usedsin2Œ∏+cos2Œ∏=1Solutionsin2Œ∏=1‚àí(257‚Äã)2sin2Œ∏=1‚àí62549‚Äãsin2Œ∏=625576‚ÄãsinŒ∏=2524‚ÄãSince Œ∏ lies in Quadrant IVsinŒ∏=‚àí2524‚ÄãFinal Answer‚àí2524‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: The mode of 5, 1, 6, 2, 6, 2, 3, 4, 24, 2, 2, 21, 2, 81 is:
questions.explanation: Given: 5, 1, 6, 2, 6, 2, 3, 4, 24, 2, 2, 21, 2, 81 Concept Used: The mode of a set of numbers is the value that appears most frequently. Solution: Let's count the frequency of each number: 1 appears 1 time 2 appears 5 times 3 appears 1 time 4 appears 1 time 5 appears 1 time 6 appears 2 times 21 appears 1 time 24 appears 1 time 81 appears 1 time The number 2 appears the most frequently (5 times).

questions.question: A and B can complete work in 9 days, while B and C can complete the same work in 18 days. When working together A, B and C, can finish the same work in 8 days. How many days will it take for A and C to complete the work together?
questions.explanation: Given: A and B can complete the work in 9 days. B and C can complete the same work in 18 days. A, B, and C together can finish the work in 8 days. We need to find how many days A and C together will take to complete the work. Solution: Let the total work be 144 units (LCM of 9, 18, and 8). Work done by A and B in 1 day = Total work / Days taken by A and B = 144 / 9 = 16 units/day Work done by B and C in 1 day = 144 / 18 = 8 units/day Work done by A, B, and C in 1 day = 144 / 8 = 18 units/day Work done by A, B, and C in 1 day = Work done by A and B + Work done by C 18 units/day = 16 units/day + C C = 18 - 16 = 2 units/day Work done by B and C in 1 day = 8 units/day 8 = B + C 8 = B + 2 B = 8 - 2 = 6 units/day Work done by A and B in 1 day = 16 units/day 16 = A + B 16 = A + 6 A = 16 - 6 = 10 units/day Work done by A and C in 1 day = A + C = 10 + 2 = 12 units/day Total work = 144 units Number of days = Total work / Work done by A and C in 1 day = 144 / 12 = 12 days Thus, A and C together can complete the work in 12 days. Exam Hall Method: ‚Äã ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/691688e6-72cf-499d-b2dc-1593465e48c0.png)

questions.question: ‚ÄãIn a blend of apple wuice and orange wuice, 20% was apple wuice. In another blend of the two wuices, 30% was orange wuice. The two blends are mixed in a certain ratio so that the ratio of apple wuice and orange wuice in the mix was 4 : 3. Find the ratio of the first blend and the second blend, in that order, in the final mix.‚Äã
questions.explanation: Given: First blend: 20% apple, 80% orange Second blend: 70% apple, 30% orange (since orange is 30%) Final mixture ratio: Apple : Orange = 4 : 3 Solution:‚Äã Apple% in mixture = (20x+70y)(x+y)\frac{(20x + 70y) }{ (x + y)}(x+y)(20x+70y)‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Orange% in mixture =(80x+30y)(x+y) \frac{(80x + 30y) }{(x + y)}(x+y)(80x+30y)‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã Condition: Apple : Orange = 4 : 3 ‚Äã(20x+70y)(80x+30y)=43\frac{(20x + 70y) }{ (80x + 30y)} =\frac 4 3(80x+30y)(20x+70y)‚Äã=34‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã 3(20x + 70y) = 4(80x + 30y) 60x + 210y = 320x + 120y 210y ‚Äì 120y = 320x ‚Äì 60x 90y = 260x ‚Äãyx=269\frac y x = \frac{ 26 } 9xy‚Äã=926‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã So, ratio of first blend : second blend = x : y = 9 : 26

questions.question: The distance between two cities X and Y is 570 Km. A train starts from X at 10 PM and travels towards Y at 110 Km/hr. Another train starts from Y at 11 PM and travels towards X at 120 Km/hr. At what time will they meet?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven: Total Distance=570 Km Train A (from X): Start 10 PM, Speed 110 Km/hr Train B (from Y): Start 11 PM, Speed 120 Km/hr Solution: Step 1: Calculate distance travelled by Train A before Train B starts. Time gap=11 PM‚àí10 PM=1 hour. Distance covered by A=110√ó1=110 Km. Remaining Distance=570‚àí110=460 Km. Step 2: Calculate Relative Speed. Relative Speed=110+120=230 Km/hr. Step 3: Calculate time to meet. Time=460230=2 hours. They meet 2 hours after 11 PM. 11:00 PM+2 hours=1:00 AM. Final Answer 1:00 AM\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Total Distance} = 570 \text{ Km} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Train A (from X): Start 10 PM, Speed 110 Km/hr} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Train B (from Y): Start 11 PM, Speed 120 Km/hr} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Step 1: Calculate distance travelled by Train A before Train B starts.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Time gap} = 11 \text{ PM} - 10 \text{ PM} = 1 \text{ hour.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Distance covered by A} = 110 \times 1 = 110 \text{ Km.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Remaining Distance} = 570 - 110 = 460 \text{ Km.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Step 2: Calculate Relative Speed.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Relative Speed} = 110 + 120 = 230 \text{ Km/hr.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Step 3: Calculate time to meet.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Time} = \frac{460}{230} = 2 \text{ hours.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{They meet 2 hours after 11 PM.} \\ \space \, \\ 11:00 \text{ PM} + 2 \text{ hours} = 1:00 \text{ AM.} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ 1:00 \text{ AM}Given: Total Distance=570 Km Train A (from X): Start 10 PM, Speed 110 Km/hr Train B (from Y): Start 11 PM, Speed 120 Km/hr Solution: Step 1: Calculate distance travelled by Train A before Train B starts. Time gap=11 PM‚àí10 PM=1 hour. Distance covered by A=110√ó1=110 Km. Remaining Distance=570‚àí110=460 Km. Step 2: Calculate Relative Speed. Relative Speed=110+120=230 Km/hr. Step 3: Calculate time to meet. Time=230460‚Äã=2 hours. They meet 2 hours after 11 PM. 11:00 PM+2 hours=1:00 AM. Final Answer 1:00 AM‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: An amount is said to double in 5 years with compound interest. How many years will it take for the amount to grow to 8 times its original value?
questions.explanation: Given: An amount doubles in 5 years under compound interest. We want to find how many years it takes to become 8 times the original amount. Solution: If the amount doubles every 5 years: Growth factor after t years = 2t/5^{t/5}t/5‚Äã‚Äã Set this equal to 8 ‚Äã2t/5=82^{t/5} = 82t/5=8‚Äã‚Äã ‚Äã2t/5=232^{t/5} = 2^32t/5=23‚Äã‚Äã Comparing both sides, ‚Äãt5=3\frac{t}{5} = 35t‚Äã=3‚Äã‚Äã t = 15 years

questions.question: The difference in the measures of two complementary angles is 24¬∞. Find the measure. of the greater angle
questions.explanation: Given: Difference in measure of two complementary angles = 24o24^o24o Concept Used: ‚Äã Sum of complementary angles are 900‚Äã Solution: Let the angles be x and y Then x+y=90ox+y = 90^ox+y=90o ...(1) ‚Äãx‚àíy=24ox - y = 24^ox‚àíy=24o ....(2) Subtracting both the equation; we get; x = 57o 57^o57o‚Äã‚Äã y = 33o33^o33o‚Äã‚Äã The greater angle is 57o57^o57o‚Äã‚Äã

questions.question: 10 litres of cold drink is added to a 50-litre mixture of water and alcohol such that the ratio of alcohol to cold drink and water is 2 : 3. Find the amount of alcohol in the solution.
questions.explanation: Given: Initial mixture of water and alcohol = 50 litres. 10 litres of cold drink is added to the mixture. The ratio of alcohol to the combined mixture of cold drink and water is 2 : 3 We need to find the amount of alcohol in the final solution. Solution: Total Final Solution Volume = 50 + 10 = 60 L The total parts in the final ratio are: Alcohol parts = 2, (Cold Drink + Water) parts = 3 So, the total number of parts is: Total parts = 2 + 3 = 5 Amount of Alcohol = 25√ó60\frac{2}{5} \times 6052‚Äã√ó60‚Äã = 24 L Thus, the amount of alcohol in the final solution is 24 litres.

questions.question: The coordinates of the vertices of a triangle are A(0,0), B(6,0), and C(0,8). What is the location of the orthocenter of the triangle?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄãGiven:A(0,0), B(6,0), C(0,8)Concept Used:In a right-angled triangle, the orthocenter is the vertex of the right angleFormula Used:Orthocenter of a right-angled triangle=vertex of the right angleSolution:mAB=0‚àí06‚àí0=0mAC=8‚àí00‚àí0=‚àûAB‚ä•ACRight angle is at A(0,0)Final Answer:(0,0)\textbf{Given:} \\A(0,0), \; B(6,0), \; C(0,8) \\[6pt]\textbf{Concept Used:} \\\text{In a right-angled triangle, the orthocenter is the vertex of the right angle} \\[6pt]\textbf{Formula Used:} \\\text{Orthocenter of a right-angled triangle} = \text{vertex of the right angle} \\[6pt]\textbf{Solution:} \\m_{AB} = \frac{0-0}{6-0} = 0 \\m_{AC} = \frac{8-0}{0-0} = \infty \\AB \perp AC \\\text{Right angle is at } A(0,0) \\[6pt]\textbf{Final Answer:} \\\boxed{(0,0)}Given:A(0,0),B(6,0),C(0,8)Concept Used:In a right-angled triangle, the orthocenter is the vertex of the right angleFormula Used:Orthocenter of a right-angled triangle=vertex of the right angleSolution:mAB‚Äã=6‚àí00‚àí0‚Äã=0mAC‚Äã=0‚àí08‚àí0‚Äã=‚àûAB‚ä•ACRight angle is at A(0,0)Final Answer:(0,0)‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã
